summary: 7 category scorers with hybrid rule mapping: metadata from BehaviorSpec, custom check logic inline
context: Phase 16 context gathering: designing how scorers map to BEHAVIORS.md rules and produce results
rationale: Category scorers (sequencing, scope quality, argument quality, decision hygiene, workflow completeness, anti-patterns, quality criteria) group logically. Hybrid approach gives traceability back to BEHAVIORS.md rule IDs while allowing sophisticated check logic that can't be expressed as rule strings. Scorers return both numeric 0-1 and pass/fail checks for thresholds and diagnostics respectively.

alternatives.option: One micro-scorer per BEHAVIORS.md rule (40+ scorers)
alternatives.reason_rejected: Too many scorers to manage, aggregation complexity, most rules are better evaluated in context of their category

alternatives.option: Fully dynamic scorers that parse rule text into checks at runtime
alternatives.reason_rejected: Rule text is natural language -- can't reliably generate check logic from it. Custom logic needed for pattern matching.
